author: potuz
content:
fwiw, this merged but just in case, I think justified hash instead of finalized one is safe <@360491619402776577> <@425572898787426305> https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/786#issuecomment-4312568113 
Even though it doesn' t change anything in the "unboundedness"  of the problem.

author: m.kalinin
content: the EL has no notion of justified, it used to be the safe block before but with FCR the safe block may not be justified anymore. from that perspective i think we should use finalized as the point of no-reorg

author: tersec
content: also finalized is the only really gasper one. everything else is a guess (yes, including fcr, kind of)

author: potuz
content: wait how can justified be a guess? but I agree with Misha that if justified is not being passed anymore as safe (actually Prysm never sent it) then it doesn't make sense.

author: potuz
content: I just felt guilty that I pushed for finalized as something that was theoretically possible when I think it may be actually impossible

author: tersec
content: it's possible to construct scenarios where one can reorg past justified

author: potuz
content: yes, but in this context those reorgs are irrelevant

author: potuz
content: the only thing that matters for this PR is the reorg to a past head and I believe this cannot happen beyond justified

author: potuz
content: but I can definitely be wrong though

author: tersec
content: ah, ok, that I'd have to think more carefully about

author: potuz
content: reorgs that are also normal reorgs in the EL side are of no concern, the issue here is reorgs in the CL that are just rollbacks from the point of view of the EL

author: potuz
content: which is a concept that apparently freaks the EL devs about

author: tersec
content: yes, the existing engine API prohibits them pointedly, I was never really sure why

author: tersec
content: like, it goes out of its way to highlight this case as invalid

author: jtraglia
content: <@586161934425128960> suggested maybe making a new alpha.6 release which contains several new/fixed tests and [5152](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5152) which is a (small) consensus breaking change. [Here](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/compare/v1.7.0-alpha.5...master) is the full list of changes since the alpha.5 release. I think we should at least wait until [the genesis handling issue](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/issues/5149) is resolved (cc <@1019999229151821936>, <@602753420033785856>, <@504202741933932544>). Also, there's a chance we run into some issue which requires a release at the beginning of the interop. Not sure if we should wait a few more days or not. Worst case we make a few releases this week.
